meaning: n.孵化,舱口,舱口盖,(门、墙壁、地板上的)开口vt.孵,孵出,策划,图谋vi.孵化
examples: The eggs quickly hatch into larvae.
He stuck his head up through the hatch.
The female must find a warm place to hatch her eggs.
The FDA had this power under the 1984 Waxman-Hatch law, designed to speed up drug approvals.
WordPress and MoveableType offer the same sort of escape hatch in their open-source based products.
